_comment: Port intelligence config. Edit freely — no code changes needed. severity: critical/high/medium/low/info

20.name: FTP Data
20.description: FTP data transfer — credentials and data sent in plaintext
20.weak_config_message: FTP data channel open — all transfers are unencrypted
20.recommendation: Replace FTP with SFTP (port 22) or FTPS

21.name: FTP Control
21.description: FTP control — credentials transmitted in cleartext
21.weak_config_message: FTP (port 21) open — username/password exposed in plaintext
21.recommendation: Disable FTP immediately. Use SFTP or FTPS instead

22.name: SSH
22.weak_config_message: SSH open — ensure key-based auth is used, not just passwords
22.recommendation: Disable password authentication, use SSH keys only. Restrict to known IPs

23.name: Telnet
23.description: Telnet — completely unencrypted shell access, credentials sent in plaintext
23.weak_config_message: CRITICAL: Telnet (port 23) open — full shell access with zero encryption
23.recommendation: Disable Telnet IMMEDIATELY. Replace with SSH. This is a critical vulnerability

25.name: SMTP
25.description: SMTP mail relay — may be abused for spam relay
25.weak_config_message: SMTP (port 25) open — check for open relay configuration
25.recommendation: Restrict SMTP to authenticated users only. Enable TLS

53.name: DNS
53.description: DNS server — may allow zone transfers or DNS amplification
53.weak_config_message: DNS (port 53) open — check for open recursive resolver or zone transfer
53.recommendation: Restrict DNS recursion to local network only. Disable zone transfers

80.name: HTTP
80.weak_config_message: HTTP (port 80) open — web traffic is unencrypted
80.recommendation: Redirect all HTTP to HTTPS. Enable HSTS

443.name: HTTPS
443.weak_config_message: HTTPS open — verify TLS version is 1.2+ and certificate is valid
443.recommendation: Ensure TLS 1.2+ is used. Disable TLS 1.0/1.1 and SSLv3

445.name: SMB
445.description: Windows file sharing (SMB) — EternalBlue/WannaCry attack vector
445.weak_config_message: CRITICAL: SMB (port 445) open — major ransomware attack vector
445.recommendation: Block SMB from internet. Apply all Windows patches. Consider disabling if not needed

554.name: RTSP
554.description: Real-Time Streaming Protocol — often IP camera streams without auth
554.weak_config_message: RTSP (port 554) open — camera stream may be accessible without authentication
554.recommendation: Enable RTSP authentication. Restrict access to local network only

631.name: IPP (Printing)
631.description: Internet Printing Protocol — may expose documents or allow unauthorized printing
631.weak_config_message: IPP (port 631) open — printer may allow unauthorized access
631.recommendation: Restrict printer access to local network. Enable authentication

1883.name: MQTT
1883.description: MQTT broker without TLS — IoT messages sent in plaintext
1883.weak_config_message: MQTT (port 1883) open without encryption — all IoT sensor data is exposed
1883.recommendation: Use MQTT over TLS on port 8883. Enable broker authentication

3306.name: MySQL/MariaDB
3306.description: MySQL database exposed on network — potential unauthorized data access
3306.weak_config_message: MySQL (port 3306) open on network — database may be accessible externally
3306.recommendation: Bind MySQL to 127.0.0.1 only. Use firewall rules. Audit user permissions

3389.name: RDP
3389.description: Remote Desktop Protocol — primary ransomware and lateral movement vector
3389.weak_config_message: CRITICAL: RDP (port 3389) exposed — most exploited Windows service
3389.recommendation: Never expose RDP to internet. Use VPN. Enable NLA. Apply all patches

5432.name: PostgreSQL
5432.weak_config_message: PostgreSQL (port 5432) open — database exposed on network
5432.recommendation: Bind to localhost only. Use pg_hba.conf to restrict connections. Audit roles

5683.name: CoAP
5683.description: Constrained Application Protocol — IoT lightweight protocol without encryption
5683.weak_config_message: CoAP (port 5683) open — IoT protocol without encryption

5900.name: VNC
5900.description: VNC remote desktop — often unencrypted, weak auth common
5900.weak_config_message: CRITICAL: VNC (port 5900) open — remote desktop with often-weak or no encryption
5900.recommendation: Disable VNC or tunnel through SSH. Use strong password. Restrict to VPN

6379.name: Redis
6379.description: Redis cache — frequently unauthenticated, allows full data access and RCE
6379.weak_config_message: CRITICAL: Redis (port 6379) open — often requires NO authentication by default
6379.recommendation: Bind to 127.0.0.1. Enable requirepass. Use firewall rules immediately

8080.name: HTTP-Alt
8080.description: Alternate HTTP port — often admin panels or proxy interfaces
8080.weak_config_message: HTTP alternate port 8080 open — check for admin interfaces without auth
8080.recommendation: Verify what service is running. Enable authentication on admin panels

8443.name: HTTPS-Alt
8443.description: Alternate HTTPS port — check TLS version and certificate validity
8443.weak_config_message: HTTPS alternate port 8443 open — verify TLS configuration

8883.name: MQTT-TLS
8883.description: Encrypted MQTT over TLS — the secure MQTT standard
8883.weak_config_message: MQTT-TLS open — verify certificate and auth are properly configured
8883.recommendation: Ensure valid certificates and client authentication are enforced

9100.name: JetDirect (Printing)
9100.description: Raw printer port — can receive arbitrary print jobs
9100.weak_config_message: JetDirect (port 9100) open — printer accepts raw jobs without authentication
9100.recommendation: Restrict to local network. Enable printer authentication

27017.name: MongoDB
27017.description: MongoDB — frequently unauthenticated, allows full DB read/write
27017.weak_config_message: CRITICAL: MongoDB (port 27017) open — may allow unauthenticated full database access
27017.recommendation: Enable MongoDB authentication. Bind to 127.0.0.1. Use firewall rules

vulnerable_firmware_patterns.description: Critically outdated OpenSSL (1.0.x) — multiple critical CVEs
Outdated PHP version — EOL, multiple RCE CVEs
Hikvision camera — verify default credentials changed
Dahua camera — verify default credentials changed
GoAhead embedded web server — extensive CVE history
Boa web server — EOL since 2005, many CVEs
